strengths: Hands-on agentic AI infrastructure work in production at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and frameworks supporting agentic workflows — directly mirrors the JD's core architectural mandate.

Demonstrated distributed-systems depth at serious scale: at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, built low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance real-time databases — the exact backend profile the role demands.

Fintech exposure (RIDE Capital investment platform) combined with a self-built event-driven geo-data platform (GISCollective on Kubernetes) shows both domain adjacency and architectural ownership of non-trivial, production systems.
concerns: No documented engineering leadership history — all roles are individual-contributor (Staff/Senior Developer); the JD requires 5+ years in senior technical leadership and a track record of scaling teams from 10 to 30+ people.

Agentic AI experience is less than one year old and described as contributory ('contribute to AI-agent infrastructure') rather than as architect or technical owner of a multi-agent system at production scale.
No evidence of operating AI or financial systems under regulatory constraints, multi-jurisdiction compliance, or board-level communication — all listed as hard requirements or key responsibilities.
response.justification: Bogdan brings genuinely rare technical substance for this role: he is actively building agentic AI infrastructure and MCP frameworks in a production context right now, and his time at Dunnhumby proves he can design and operate distributed systems at serious commercial scale — the two hardest technical bars in the JD. A company willing to bet on a high-ceiling technical hire rather than a polished executive profile will find a craftsman who already lives inside the problem space; the leadership gap is real but bridgeable with the right support structure around the CTO seat.
